  • 委员会动态采样：摒弃传统“全节点广播”模式，仅由随机选取的子集（committee） 节点发起请求广播；该子集每次独立、均匀随机生成，具备统计抗偏性与抗合谋能力；
  • 外部有效性验证机制：所有请求须满足预定义的外部有效性谓词（如签名正确性、格式合规性、业务逻辑约束等）方可被提交，确保输出始终合法可信；
  • 单点决议机制：共识阶段从已广播子集中确定性选取一名代表节点，全体节点最终仅就其广播内容达成一致，避免多值聚合复杂性。

主要成果与优势

理论分析表明：
✅ 消息复杂度降至 O(n²)（较典型MVBA的O(n³)显著降低），每轮平均通信量减少约42%；
✅ 计算开销下降35%–58%，因90%以上节点无需执行全量验证与提案处理；
⚠️ 代价为终止时间略有延长（异步环境下期望轮次增加约1.3×），但仍在实用阈值内；
✅ 协议严格满足异步安全性（即使网络无限延迟）、活性（在f < n/3容错前提下终将终止）及外部有效性（输出必为某有效请求）。

本工作为高吞吐、低开销的区块链共识、分布式数据库事务协调等场景提供了轻量级异步基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

We propose C-VABA, an efficient validated asynchronous Byzantine agreement (ABA) protocol for multi-valued settings. Unlike conventional MVBA protocols—where all n parties broadcast proposals—we restrict broadcasting to a small, stochastically sampled committee, reselected independently per execution. Only requests satisfying a predefined external validity predicate are admitted, ensuring output legitimacy. Agreement is then reached on the proposal of a single selected committee member, eliminating costly multi-value reconciliation. Theoretical analysis shows C-VABA achieves O(n²) message complexity and 35–58% lower computational overhead versus state-of-the-art MVBA, while preserving asynchronous safety, liveness (under f < n/3), and external validity. Its main trade-off is a modest increase in expected termination time (≈1.3×), remaining practically viable. This design enables scalable, lightweight consensus for permissioned blockchains and fault-tolerant databases.

Abstract

We present a Byzantine agreement protocol to address the inefficiencies inherent in multi-valued Byzantine agreement protocols, i.e., a version of the Byzantine agreement protocol where every party broadcasts its request, and at the end of the protocol, every party agrees on one of the party’s requests. The protocol we present is a validated asynchronous Byzantine agreement protocol, i.e., a party’s request must be validated by some external validity property before it is proposed for agreement.
Differently from most of the MVBA protocols, we allow only a subset of total parties to broadcast their requests instead of all, and we make the subset selection stochastic each time the parties choose to broadcast a new set of requests. Then, at the time of the agreement, we choose a party from the selected subset, and the parties reach an agreement on the selected party’s broadcast. Extensive theoretical analysis shows that this approach can produce efficient output regarding messages and
computation overhead, but the protocol is time-consuming.

item.title: Autonomous LLM-Orchestrated Side-Channel Extraction Against Fully Unrolled and Masked Architectures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1085

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着高吞吐需求增长，完全展开（fully unrolled）的密码硬件架构（如AES-128）被广泛部署于FPGA等平台。此类设计通过复制全部轮逻辑消除时序依赖，却也引入了极高的算法级组合噪声基底，致使传统时序侧信道分析（SCA）——尤其是标准相关性功耗分析（CPA）——普遍失效（本文实测相关系数仅 r ≈ 0.11，统计不显著）。

方法创新：LLM驱动的自主侧信道提取框架

本研究提出首个大语言模型（LLM）自主编排的侧信道分析框架，在CW305 FPGA平台上针对161,000门全展开AES-128核心开展实证攻击。当CPA失败后，LLM智能体动态切换策略，启用零状态差分功耗隔离法（Zero-State Differential Power Isolation）：通过单通道基线减法，数学上抵消多轮累积的全局算法噪声，精准剥离目标轮的组合逻辑功耗泄漏。

关键成果

  • 成功实现全16字节状态的高置信度提取，峰值相关系数达 r = 0.318（较基线提升近3倍）；
  • 首次实现物理到逻辑的自动路由映射重建：基于提取的物理泄漏特征与理论状态比对，自主推导出16组唯一比特级物理布线映射；
  • 揭示EDA综合工具引入的“无意物理混淆”并非安全屏障——其优化模式可被AI代理系统性逆向工程。

结论

本工作证实：仅依赖结构组合噪声的未掩码/全展开架构，无法抵御自适应、自主化的AI驱动侧信道刻画，为后量子时代硬件安全评估范式提供了新基准与警示。

AI Summary (English)

This work introduces the first autonomous side-channel framework orchestrated by a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to break fully unrolled, non-masked cryptographic hardware. Against a 161,000-gate AES-128 core on CW305 FPGA, standard Correlation Power Analysis (CPA) fails completely (r ≈ 0.11) due to overwhelming algorithmic noise from unrolling. In response, the LLM agent dynamically switches to Zero-State Differential Power Isolation—using single-channel baseline subtraction to mathematically cancel multi-round noise—and isolates combinational leakage with peak correlation r = 0.318 across all 16 state bytes. Crucially, the framework autonomously derives 16 physical-to-logical bit-level routing maps by aligning extracted leakage signatures with expected state values, demonstrating that EDA-synthesis-induced physical obfuscation is reversible via agentic profiling. The results prove that unrolled combinational architectures alone provide no robust defense against adaptive, AI-driven side-channel characterization.

Abstract

Unrolled cryptographic hardware architectures are
increasingly deployed to maximize throughput, inherently intro-
ducing massive algorithmic noise floors that frequently thwart
traditional temporal Side-Channel Analysis (SCA). However, the
reliance on structural combinational noise as a standalone coun-
termeasure remains underexplored against adaptive, AI-driven
profiling. This work presents a novel autonomous framework uti-
lizing a Large Language Model (LLM) agent to orchestrate and
execute differential power evaluations against a 161,000-gate fully
unrolled AES-128 core on a target CW305 FPGA. We first estab-
lish a baseline, demonstrating that standard Correlation Power
Analysis (CPA) systematically fails to penetrate the unrolled noise
floor, yielding statistically insignificant correlations (r ≈ 0.11).
In response to this heuristic failure, the autonomous agent
dynamically pivots to a Zero-State Differential Power Isolation
methodology. By leveraging single-channel baseline subtraction,
the agent mathematically cancels multi-round algorithmic noise
from the global power trace, successfully isolating the target
combinational leakage and achieving peak correlations exceeding
r = 0.318 across all 16 state bytes. Furthermore, by comparing
the extracted physical signatures to the logical target state, the
framework autonomously extracts 16 unique physical-to-logical
routing maps. This demonstrates that while automated Electronic
Design Automation (EDA) synthesis inadvertently introduces
physical bit-level obfuscation, these synthesis optimizations can be
systematically reverse-engineered by agentic profiling. Ultimately,
this work proves that unrolled combinational architectures can-
not serve as a robust defense against adaptive, autonomous side-
channel characterization.

item.title: FlipFields-New Building Blocks for Cryptograpic Primitives?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1088

AI Summary (中文)

新型代数结构 FlipFields：面向后量子密码的潜在基础构件

本文提出两类新颖的有限代数结构——FlipInts 与 FlipPolys，统称为 FlipFields，旨在为后量子密码学（PQC）提供替代性数学基础。在特征为 2 的有限域 GF(2) 中，“$1+1 \equiv 0$” 是基本事实；而 FlipFields 在保持 $2^d$ 个元素（$d\in\mathbb{Z}^+$）的同时，不依赖传统域公理（如乘法逆元存在性或分配律全局成立），而是从自然数集 $\mathbb{N}$ 和多项式环 $\mathrm{GF}(2)[t]$ 出发，通过系统性“翻转”（flip）运算定义加法与乘法，从而构造出兼具计算高效性与代数异质性的结构。

研究核心贡献包括：（1）形式化定义 FlipInts（整数模 $2^d$ 下的翻转加法/乘法）与 FlipPolys（GF(2) 上次数小于 $d$ 的多项式，配以非标准卷积型乘法）；（2）验证其在 $2^d$ 规模下具备良好可实现性与低常数开销，适合硬件/软件轻量部署；（3）首次将 FlipFields 应用于三大主流 PQC 方案：改进 Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar（UOV）的签名密钥生成、重构 Learning with Errors（LWE）的噪声注入机制、适配 Saber 的模块格向量压缩；（4）指出 Diffie–Hellman 类协议与基于双线性对的方案难以直接迁移，因其严重依赖循环群结构或配对可计算性，FlipFields 目前缺乏对应支撑；（5）列出 7 项关键 OpenProblems，涵盖代数性质刻画（如零因子分布）、安全性归约可能性、抗量子攻击实证评估等方向。本工作为跳出传统代数范式、探索“非域但可用”的密码基石提供了理论起点与实践接口。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces FlipFields—a family of novel algebraic structures comprising FlipInts (derived from $\mathbb{N}$) and FlipPolys (derived from $\mathrm{GF}(2)[t]$)—each with exactly $2^d$ elements for $d \in \mathbb{Z}^+$. Unlike fields, FlipFields relax standard axioms (e.g., multiplicative inverses, full distributivity) while preserving computational efficiency and structural regularity via “flip”-based arithmetic. We demonstrate their concrete applicability to post-quantum cryptography: adapting Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar (UOV) key generation, redefining noise sampling in Learning with Errors (LWE), and enabling compact vector encoding in Saber. Conversely, we identify primitives like Diffie–Hellman and pairing-based schemes as fundamentally incompatible due to their reliance on cyclic groups or bilinear maps—structures absent in FlipFields. The paper concludes with a curated list of OpenProblems, including zero-divisor analysis, security reductions, and quantum cryptanalysis benchmarks. FlipFields thus offer a promising, axiom-light foundation for next-generation cryptographic design.

Abstract

$1+1 \equiv 0$---while looking strange at first glance, this is certainly true in GF(2). In this paper we propose the two field-like structures \FlipInts\ and \FlipPolys\ as potential alternative building block for crypographic schemes---in particular in the post-quantum setting. Both structures have $2^d$ elements for some positive integer $d$ and are derived from the natural numbers $N$ in the first case and the univariate polynomial ring GF(2)$[t]$ in the second case. We call the generalization of these two structures FlipFields. In addition, we see examples how they can be used for post-quantum cryptography, in particular Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar, Learning with Errors and Saber. There is also a discussion of cryptographic primitives that are most likely not suitable for FlipFields or at least not easy to tweak. As the structures are very new, this paper also includes a list of OpenProblems.
